key_themes: Moderate Buy analyst consensus with ~9% upside
summary: Verizon (VZ) sentiment is solidly positive in mid-2026, driven by a sharp stock rally of over 24% YTD following a Q4 earnings beat, significantly raised full-year guidance for EPS ($4.90–$4.95), subscriber adds (750k–1M postpaid phones), and 2–3% service revenue growth. The ~5.9–6% dividend yield remains the dominant retail attraction for income investors, complemented by 5G sponsorship of the FIFA World Cup 2026, a $25B buyback, and an improving growth narrative under new leadership. Analysts largely rate it Moderate Buy/Outperform with average price targets near $51 (8–10% upside from ~$46.60), though some retail and analyst commentary flags limited long-term growth, disclosure changes, and a recent congressional stock sale as cautionary notes.
sources: 25+ recent news articles (Motley Fool, Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, Zacks), 20+ X posts (Latest and semantic search since Mar 2026), Analyst ratings from 20+ firms (avg PT ~$51), Reddit dividend community discussions
